novel_features.name: Project-scoped Memory Admin
novel_features.description: Scope every CLI and MCP request to one Supermemory project with the x-sm-project header, without hand-editing raw headers.
novel_features.rationale: Supermemory project scoping is documented for MCP users but is not represented as a first-class OpenAPI parameter; operators need a safe per-project boundary for daily agent memory work.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Keeps agent memories partitioned by project/codebase while preserving the same CLI surface.

novel_features.name: Recall Search for Agents
novel_features.description: Run low-latency memory recall from a compact, agent-friendly CLI surface with selectable JSON output.
novel_features.rationale: The API exposes recall, but the generated CLI turns it into a repeatable terminal/MCP workflow with auth setup, dry runs, output selection, and local validation.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets agents retrieve focused memory context without opening a dashboard or carrying broad history in prompt context.

novel_features.name: Local Memory Operations Cache
novel_features.description: Sync compatible Supermemory resources into local SQLite for offline search and inspection.
novel_features.rationale: A local cache lets agents inspect recent memory operations without repeatedly hitting live API endpoints or carrying large response payloads in context.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Gives operators a local audit/search loop for memory-adjacent resources.

narrative.headline: A project-scoped admin CLI for Supermemory memory, recall, documents, profiles, and container tags.
narrative.value_prop: Supermemory's API is powerful, but agents need a repeatable operator surface: auth setup, project scoping, dry runs, compact JSON output, local sync/search, and MCP parity.
narrative.auth_narrative: Create a Supermemory API key and set SUPERMEMORY_ADMIN_TOKEN. Optionally set SUPERMEMORY_ADMIN_PROJECT to scope every request with x-sm-project.

quickstart.comment: Verify config, base URL reachability, and auth hints.
Preview the recall request body without sending credentials or making a live call.
Preview a project-scoped profile request using the x-sm-project header.
